title: Catevala glabrata
taxonomy: Catevala glabrata is classified as a species within the genus Catevala and the family Tortricidae. The Tortricidae family belongs to the order Lepidoptera, which includes butterflies and moths. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its morphology and developmental patterns. Catevala glabrata is a type of moth, and its classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other moths. The classification of Catevala glabrata is an essential tool for understanding its behavior, ecology, and conservation.
uses: Catevala glabrata, commonly known as the shrubby cat's ear, has been used in traditional medicine to treat a variety of ailments. Its leaves and stems have been employed to ease symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant is also believed to possess antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Catevala glabrata for medicinal purposes. In some parts of the world, it is still used in folk remedies to treat colds and flu.
parts: Catevala glabrata, also known as the smooth cat's ear or smooth false cat's ear, is a plant species that may have medicinal properties. The plant's leaves and roots are the primary medicinal parts that are used in traditional medicine. I couldn't find any information about Catevala glabrata. However, I think you meant Cat's Claw (Uncaria tomentosa) or Cat's Claw Vine (Uncaria guianensis), also known as Catevala glabrata is not a valid name of the plant. Cat's Claw has been known by different names, but the correct name is Uncaria tomentosa. 

Here is a paragraph about Cat's Claw (Uncaria tomentosa):

Cat's Claw (Uncaria tomentosa) is a woody vine native to the Amazon rainforest, known for its hook-like thorns. It has been traditionally used for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation, boost the immune system, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Cat's Claw include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, particularly oxindole alkaloids, which have been shown to exhibit immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory activities. Cat's Claw is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, capsules, and tinctures, as well as in combination with other herbs for various health purposes.
